title: Protein quantification by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie  staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"at0ii","text":"This protocol provides a step-by-step guide for quantifying protein concentration by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining. It details the preparation of samples and BSA standards, protein separation via electrophoresis, staining, imaging, and data analysis for accurate protein quantification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"adrlp","text":"Follow institutional guidelines for the disposal of biological and chemical waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5qavi","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"46th8","text":"β-mercaptoethanol (Cat# M3148, Sigma)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpskm","text":"BSA (Cat# A4161, Sigma)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f83rf","text":"NuPAGETM LDS Sample Buffer (4X) (Cat# NP0007, Invitrogen)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":6,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ojrj","text":"NuPAGETM 4–12% Bis-Tris precast gels (Cat# NP0321, Cat# NP0322, Cat# NP0322; Invitrogen)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":6,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9a8vr","text":"NuPAGETM MOPS SDS Running Buffer (20X) (Cat# NP0001; Invitrogen)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":6,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5eiq","text":"SeeBlueTM Plus2 Pre-stained Protein Standard (Cat# LC5925, Invitrogen)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":7,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8r842","text":"InstantBlue‱ Coomassie Protein Stain (ISB1L) (Cat# ab119211, abcam)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":11,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7613g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"83E3660FB96111EF95DA0A58A9FEAC02","name":":registered:"}}}}
steps: Prepare protein samples ( 12 µL ) with 4 µL LDS sample loading buffer (supplemented with β-mercaptoethanol).
Load prepared standards and protein samples onto a precast NuPAGE 4-12% BisTris gel, alongside molecular weight markers for reference.
Run the gel for 01:00:00 at 170 V, Room temperature . Use MOPS running buffer.
Stain the gel with Coomassie for approximately 01:00:00 or until bands are clearly visible.
Analyze protein band intensities using ImageJ software ( http://fiji.sc ; RRID:SCR_002285).
Destain the gel using distilled water until the background is clear and bands are distinct.
Prepare BSA standards ( 12 µL of 0.01 mg/ml, 0.02 mg/ml, 0.04 mg/ml, 0.08 mg/ml, 0.1 mg/ml) with 4 µL LDS sample loading buffer (supplemented with β-mercaptoethanol).
Capture an image of the stained gel using an ordinary scanner or Bio-Rad ChemiDoc MP imaging system.

Compare the intensity of the protein bands to the standard curve. Calculate the concentration of the protein of interest based on its band intensity and the curve. Ensure that the protein sample is within the linear range of the standard curve for accurate quantification.
Plot the intensity of the BSA bands as a function of protein concentration. Generate a standard curve using these data points.
creator: Sarah van Veen
